One additional point to support my agreement is that while hyperopia might affect overall focus clarity regardless of neurological impairment, it does not specifically correlate with the mechanical limitations imposed by trochlear nerve palsy. Specifically, since the trochlear nerve primarily influences vertical eye movement via the superior oblique muscle, any deviation towards horizontal alignment would more likely indicate another type of cranial nerve issue, thus ruling out exotropia as well.
